root_text.ud2.10:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā anupiyāyaṁ viharati ambavane.
root_text.ud2.10:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā bhaddiyo kāḷīgodhāya putto araññagatopi rukkhamūlagatopi suññāgāragatopi abhikkhaṇaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.ud2.10:2.1: Assosuṁ kho sambahulā bhikkhū āyasmato bhaddiyassa kāḷīgodhāya puttassa araññagatassapi rukkhamūlagatassapi suññāgāragatassapi abhikkhaṇaṁ udānaṁ udānentassa:
root_text.ud2.10:2.4: “nissaṁsayaṁ kho, āvuso, āyasmā bhaddiyo kāḷīgodhāya putto anabhirato brahmacariyaṁ carati,
root_text.ud2.10:2.5: yaṁsa pubbe agāriyabhūtassa rajjasukhaṁ, so tamanussaramāno araññagatopi rukkhamūlagatopi suññāgāragatopi abhikkhaṇaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.ud2.10:3.1: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.ud2.10:3.2: “āyasmā, bhante, bhaddiyo kāḷīgodhāya putto araññagatopi rukkhamūlagatopi suññāgāragatopi abhikkhaṇaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.ud2.10:3.4: Nissaṁsayaṁ kho, bhante, āyasmā bhaddiyo kāḷīgodhāya putto anabhirato brahmacariyaṁ carati.
root_text.ud2.10:3.5: Yaṁsa pubbe agāriyabhūtassa rajjasukhaṁ, so tamanussaramāno araññagatopi rukkhamūlagatopi suññāgāragatopi abhikkhaṇaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.ud2.10:4.1: Atha kho bhagavā aññataraṁ bhikkhuṁ āmantesi:
root_text.ud2.10:4.2: “ehi tvaṁ, bhikkhu, mama vacanena bhaddiyaṁ bhikkhuṁ āmantehi:
root_text.ud2.10:5.1: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho so bhikkhu bhagavato paṭissutvā yenāyasmā bhaddiyo kāḷīgodhāya putto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhaddiyaṁ kāḷīgodhāya puttaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.10:5.3: “Evamāvuso”ti kho āyasmā bhaddiyo kāḷīgodhāya putto tassa bhikkhuno paṭissutvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ bhaddiyaṁ kāḷīgodhāya puttaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.ud2.10:6.1: “Saccaṁ kira tvaṁ, bhaddiya, araññagatopi rukkhamūlagatopi suññāgāragatopi abhikkhaṇaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.ud2.10:7.1: “Kiṁ pana tvaṁ, bhaddiya, atthavasaṁ sampassamāno araññagatopi rukkhamūlagatopi suññāgāragatopi abhikkhaṇaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.ud2.10:7.3: “Pubbe me, bhante, agāriyabhūtassa rajjaṁ kārentassa antopi antepure rakkhā susaṁvihitā ahosi, bahipi antepure rakkhā susaṁvihitā ahosi, antopi nagare rakkhā susaṁvihitā ahosi, bahipi nagare rakkhā susaṁvihitā ahosi, antopi janapade rakkhā susaṁvihitā ahosi, bahipi janapade rakkhā susaṁvihitā ahosi.
root_text.ud2.10:7.4: So kho ahaṁ, bhante, evaṁ rakkhito gopito santo bhīto ubbiggo ussaṅkī utrāsī vihāsiṁ.
root_text.ud2.10:7.5: Etarahi kho panāhaṁ, bhante, araññagatopi rukkhamūlagatopi suññāgāragatopi eko abhīto anubbiggo anussaṅkī anutrāsī appossukko pannalomo paradattavutto, migabhūtena cetasā viharāmi.
root_text.ud2.10:7.6: Imaṁ kho ahaṁ, bhante, atthavasaṁ sampassamāno araññagatopi rukkhamūlagatopi suññāgāragatopi abhikkhaṇaṁ udānaṁ udānesiṁ:
root_text.ud2.10:8.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud2.10:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Anupiya in a mango grove.
translation_text.ud2.10:1.3: Now at that time, Venerable Bhaddiya son of Kāḷīgodhā, even in the wilderness, at the foot of a tree, or in an empty dwelling, frequently expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud2.10:2.4: “Without a doubt, Venerable Bhaddiya leads the spiritual life dissatisfied.
translation_text.ud2.10:2.5: It’s when recalling the pleasures of royalty he formerly enjoyed as a layperson that, even in the wilderness, at the foot of a tree, or in an empty dwelling, he frequently expresses this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud2.10:3.1: Then those mendicants went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what was happening.
translation_text.ud2.10:4.1: So the Buddha addressed one of the monks,
translation_text.ud2.10:4.2: “Please, monk, in my name tell the mendicant Bhaddiya that
translation_text.ud2.10:5.1: “Yes, sir,” that monk replied. He went to Bhaddiya and said to him,
translation_text.ud2.10:5.3: “Yes, reverend,” Bhaddiya replied. He went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.ud2.10:6.1: “Is it really true, Bhaddiya, that even in the wilderness, at the foot of a tree, or in an empty dwelling, you frequently express this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud2.10:7.3: “Formerly, as a layperson ruling the land, my guard was well organized within and without the royal compound, within and without the city, and within and without the country.
translation_text.ud2.10:7.4: But although I was guarded and defended in this way, I remained fearful, scared, suspicious, and nervous.
translation_text.ud2.10:7.5: But these days, even when alone in the wilderness, at the foot of a tree, or in an empty dwelling, I’m not fearful, scared, suspicious, or nervous. I live relaxed, unruffled, surviving on charity, my heart free as a wild deer.
translation_text.ud2.10:7.6: It is for this reason that, even in the wilderness, at the foot of a tree, or in an empty dwelling, I frequently expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud2.10:8.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud2.10:9.2: passed beyond any kind of existence;
translation_text.ud2.10:9.3: happy, free from fear and sorrow—
translation_text.ud2.10:9.4: even the gods can’t see them.”

variant_text.ud2.10:7.5: eko → ekako (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1) | paradattavutto → paradavutto (si, sya-all, pts-vp-pli1)
variant_text.ud2.10:7.6: Imaṁ → idaṁ (bj, mr) | udānesiṁ → udānemi (bj, mr)
